# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Release package

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  build-and-release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - name: Set up Python/Pip for Windows
        uses: actions/setup-python@v2
        with:
          python-version: '3.x'
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install .
      - name: Create GitHub Release and publish package
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          NPM_USERNAME: ${NPM_USERNAME}
          NPM_PASSWORD: ${NPM_PASSWORD}
          PYPI_USERNAME: ${PYPI_USERNAME}
          PYPI_PASSWORD: ${PYPI_PASSWORD}
        run: |
          if [ "$GITHUB_REF" == "refs/heads/main" ]; then
            echo "Release on main branch, skipping publishing"
          else
            python -m pip install twine
            python -m twine upload --repository-url https://test.pypi.org/legacy/ dist/*
            python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel
            twine upload dist/*
          fi

          gh auth login \
            --with-token ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} \
            --scopes read:repo,write:repo \
            --endpoint https://api.github.com

          gh release create \
            --title "$GITHUB_REF_NAME" \
            --notes "Release $GITHUB_REF_NAME"

      - name: Create tags
        run: |
          git tag -a v$GITHUB_REF_NAME -m "v$GITHUB_REF_NAME"
          git push origin :v$GITHUB_REF_NAME
